title: Security audit
description: Do a comprehensive security audit of this repo.
close_reason: Superseded by recent audit; findings tracked as individual issues (timing attack fix, CSP, etc.)

title: Voice agent guardrails: redirect drift + data-leak, end on repeat offence
description:
Voice agents need to stay on-topic during calls. Two failure modes exist: (1) data leak — agent volunteers information outside `allowed_to_share`; (2) conversation drift — recipient pulls agent off the call's `goal`. Neither is currently guarded against in `VapiAdapter#build_system_prompt`.

## Design decisions

- **Off-topic covers**: both data leak and conversation drift
- **Response pattern**: redirect up to `max_redirects` times (default 2), then end the call gracefully
- **Goodbye script** (hardcoded for now): "I'm not able to help with that — I'll let you go. Have a great day."
- **Guardrail location**: system prompt (redirect instructions) + Vapi assistant config (`endCallFunctionEnabled: true`, `endCallMessage`)
- **Outcome status**: new value `terminated_off_topic` — distinct from `declined` / `failed`
- **New `CallPlan` field**: `max_redirects` (integer, default 2)
- **Always on**: every `CallPlan` gets guardrails — no opt-out flag
design: Prompt injection approach chosen over Vapi's native topic-restriction (no such feature exists). `endCallFunctionEnabled` gives the LLM a declared tool to hang up cleanly. `endCallPhrases` rejected — too brittle (exact phrase matching). Escalation path (`EscalationNotifier`) explicitly not used — too heavy for off-topic.
acceptance_criteria:
- `CallPlan` has `max_redirects` integer column (migration), default 2
- `VapiAdapter#build_system_prompt` injects a guardrail section anchored to `goal`, `allowed_to_share`, and `max_redirects`
- Vapi assistant config sets `endCallFunctionEnabled: true` and `endCallMessage` to the hardcoded goodbye
- `OutcomeExtractor` (and its prompt) recognises `terminated_off_topic` as a valid outcome status
- `Call` outcome enum includes `terminated_off_topic`
- Unit specs: guardrail prompt present in system prompt, correct `endCallFunctionEnabled`/`endCallMessage` in assistant config, `terminated_off_topic` outcome parsed correctly
- `bin/rubocop --parallel` clean; `bundle exec parallel_rspec spec/` green
close_reason: Implemented in feature/guardrails — all AC met

title: User navigation menu: access delegations and notification settings
description:
## Problem

Users have no navigation menu to move between key areas of the app. There is no way to get to /settings/notifications from the UI, and no persistent nav for accessing delegations or other sections.

## Desired state

A persistent nav element (header or sidebar) visible to all authenticated users with links to:
- Delegations (existing /delegations)
- Notification Settings (/settings/notifications)
- Any other top-level user pages added in future

## Notes

- The layout already has a top nav bar (app/views/layouts/application.html.erb) — extend it rather than adding a second nav
- Should be responsive (mobile-friendly)
- Active link highlighting is a nice-to-have

title: CallSession transcript: chat-style layout (left / right bubbles)
description:
## Goal

Refresh the **CallSession transcript** UI so it reads like a familiar chat thread: one party’s messages aligned **right** (e.g. Agent / AI), the other aligned **left** (e.g. Staff / Recipient / User), with bubble-style containers instead of a single left-aligned column.

## Context

- Transcript is rendered from `app/views/call_sessions/_transcript.html.erb` (included from `call_sessions/show.html.erb`).
- Lines are already parsed into turns with `role` (`:agent` vs `:recipient` / `:unknown`) and optional `speaker` labels (`Agent`, `Staff`, `User`, `Recipient` — see regex in the partial).
- Today both sides render as a **left-aligned** list with avatars; we want a clearer **conversation** metaphor.

## Scope (suggested)

- [ ] **Full transcript** (`_transcript.html.erb`): chat bubbles, max-width per message, left vs right alignment by `role`, keep dark mode and existing typography scale where possible.
- [ ] **Grouping**: consecutive messages from the same role should visually group (no repeated header every line) — current `show_label` / `prev_role` logic can inform this.
- [ ] **Unknown / unparsed lines**: sensible fallback (e.g. neutral system-style block or left-aligned) so we never drop content.
- [ ] **Optional follow-up**: `call_sessions/_live_status.html.erb` shows a clipped “Live transcript”; only adjust if we want consistency (could stay minimal).

## Acceptance criteria

- Agent (AI) messages appear on one side; human / business side on the other, in a layout users recognize as “chat”.
- Accessible contrast; readable on mobile (narrow column, bubbles don’t overflow).
- No change to transcript **storage** or parsing contract unless we discover a bug — UI-only unless necessary.

## Out of scope

- Changing Vapi/webhook transcript format (unless required for correct speaker detection).

title: Notification channels: per-user credentials + multi-channel support (Pushover, Telegram)
description:
## Problem

Pushover credentials are currently global (credentials.yml / ENV vars). Every user gets notified via the same account, which breaks in a multi-user system.

Additionally, the escalation notifier is Pushover-only with no abstraction. Adding new channels requires invasive changes.

## Desired state

1. **Per-user notification credentials** — each user stores their own channel tokens (Pushover user_key, Telegram chat_id, etc.) so notifications land in the right place.
2. **Channel abstraction** — `EscalationNotifier` dispatches through a channel strategy; adding a new channel (e.g. Telegram) requires only a new strategy class.
3. **Telegram support** — first new channel after Pushover, using the Telegram Bot API.

## Proposed approach

- Add a `notification_channels` table (user, channel_type enum, encrypted_credentials jsonb, enabled bool)
- Or simpler MVP: add per-channel columns to `users` (pushover_user_key, telegram_chat_id) and a `notification_channels` preference field
- Extract `Notifiers::PushoverChannel` and `Notifiers::TelegramChannel` behind a common interface
- `EscalationNotifier` iterates enabled channels for the user and fires in parallel (consistent with ADR-0008 belt-and-suspenders pattern)

## Schema sketch (notifications_channels table approach)

```
user_id        bigint FK
channel_type   string (pushover | telegram | sms)
credentials    jsonb (encrypted)
enabled        boolean default true
```

## Open questions

- Store per-channel credentials in the DB (encrypted) or ask users to configure via a settings UI?
- Should channel ordering / priority matter, or always fire all enabled channels in parallel?
- Telegram bot: shared app bot or user-created bot token?
close_reason: Implemented: notification_channels table, PushoverChannel + TelegramChannel strategies, EscalationNotifier refactored to dispatch all enabled user channels in parallel, settings UI at /settings/notifications. All 239 specs green.

title: Visual redesign + real-time streaming of call transcript on session show page
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Current State

The transcript section in `app/views/call_sessions/show.html.erb` renders the raw transcript string inside a `<details>` collapse as `whitespace-pre-wrap` plain text. It has no visual hierarchy, no speaker identity, and no live updates — the page must be manually refreshed to see new lines appear mid-call.

The transcript format is already structured (`"Agent: ...\nStaff: ...\n"` speaker-prefixed lines), so the raw material for a proper chat-log UI exists. The Turbo Stream subscription (`turbo_stream_from [@delegation, :call_sessions]`) and the transcript DOM target (`id="call_session_transcript_ID"`) are already wired on the page — the backend just never broadcasts to them.

## Two Work Items

### 1 — Visual redesign (frontend-design pass)

Replace the `<details>` plaintext block with a styled conversation log:

- **Parse speaker turns** — split on `\n`, extract `Speaker: text` into structured turn objects. Fall back gracefully for unparseable lines.
- **Turn grouping** — consecutive lines from the same speaker collapse under one avatar; only the first line in a run shows the speaker label.
- **Speaker identity** — Agent turns: brand-blue monospace label + slightly bolder text. Recipient turns: slate label + normal weight. Avatar icons (sparkle for AI, person for human) appear on the first line of each run.
- **Typography** — speaker labels in `DM Mono`, uppercase, tight tracking; body copy in `DM Sans`. Consistent with the existing design system.
- **Empty / loading state** — replace the `italic` fallback with a centered icon + "No transcript yet." that feels intentional.
- **Open by default** — the `<details>` should start open; the chevron toggle remains for collapsing.
- Use the existing `.card` component and design tokens (`--font-mono`, `--font-display`, brand/slate palette) throughout.

Key files: `app/views/call_sessions/show.html.erb`

### 2 — Real-time streaming mid-call

The transcript target div (`id="call_session_transcript_ID"`) sits inside the Turbo Stream subscription but `CallSession` only broadcasts on `status` and `outcome` changes — not on `transcript` changes.

**What needs to happen:**

1. Add `after_update_commit :broadcast_transcript_update, if: :saved_change_to_transcript?` to `CallSession`.
2. Implement `broadcast_transcript_update` to replace the transcript div via `broadcast_replace_to([@delegation, :call_sessions], target: "call_session_transcript_ID", ...)` — similar pattern to `broadcast_outcome_update`.
3. The partial rendered into the target should be the same styled turn list from item 1 so live and post-call views are identical.
4. `WebhookProcessor#handle_transcript` already appends each `final` transcript chunk via `update!` on every Vapi `transcript` webhook event — so the broadcast will fire naturally per chunk with no webhook changes needed.

Key files:
- `app/models/call_session.rb` — add callback + method
- New partial (e.g. `app/views/call_sessions/_transcript.html.erb`) — extracted from show so both the initial render and Turbo Stream replace share the same template

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The transcript section renders as a chat-log with visually distinct Agent vs. recipient turns.
- [ ] Speaker labels use `DM Mono`, turn body uses `DM Sans`.
- [ ] Consecutive turns from the same speaker are visually grouped (one avatar per run).
- [ ] Empty state is a polished placeholder, not raw italic text.
- [ ] During an active call, new transcript lines stream in without a page refresh.
- [ ] After the call ends, the final transcript is identical in appearance to the live view.
- [ ] Dark mode works throughout.
- [ ] No regressions to the Turbo Stream status/outcome updates.

## Notes

- The live transcript preview in `_live_status.html.erb` (shown on the delegations dashboard) is separate and out of scope here — that can be a follow-on.
- Transcript format is a plain `text` column (not JSONB), so the speaker parsing must happen in the view/partial, not at the model level.

title: Hang up
description: We need the agent to hang up the call when confident the call is ending.

title: Enable Content Security Policy
description:
The CSP initializer is fully commented out in `config/initializers/content_security_policy.rb`. No CSP header is sent to browsers, leaving XSS surface open on the UI.

Enable a baseline policy covering `default_src`, `script_src`, `style_src`, `img_src`, `object_src`.

title: Fix webhook signature timing attack vulnerability
description:
The webhook signature check in `WebhooksController` uses string equality which is vulnerable to timing attacks.

```ruby
# Current (vulnerable):
request.headers["Authorization"] == "Bearer #{secret}"

# Fix:
ActiveSupport::SecurityUtils.secure_compare(
  request.headers["Authorization"].to_s,
  "Bearer #{secret}"
)
```

File: `app/controllers/webhooks_controller.rb:19`

title: More templates
description: Build out 3 more use case templates for calls

title: Add option to schedule calls for a future date/time
description:
## Problem
Calls are currently started immediately after approval, but users need the ability to schedule calls to run later.

## Proposed solution
Add a scheduling option so a user can choose a future date and time with timezone handling before a call session is enqueued.

## Scope
- Add a scheduled_at field or equivalent to call execution flow
- UI: allow choosing immediate vs scheduled call
- Enqueue PlaceCallJob for future execution when scheduled
- Show scheduled status in relevant views
- Preserve current immediate-call behavior as default

## Acceptance criteria
- User can schedule a call for a future time from the call plan flow
- Scheduled calls are executed at or after the selected time
- Timezone behavior is explicit and tested
- Scheduled calls can be seen in UI with clear status
- Existing immediate-call path remains unchanged

## Notes
- Consider guardrails for scheduling in the past
- Consider editing or canceling scheduled calls in a follow-up issue if needed

title: Separate EscalationNotifier from timeout scheduling: single responsibility for notifier
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

`EscalationNotifier#notify` uses an `ensure` block to both mark `notified_at` and enqueue `EscalationTimeoutJob`. A class named "Notifier" secretly controls the escalation timeout lifecycle. The timeout always schedules — even when the push notification fails — and the scheduling is invisible to the code that creates the escalation (`WebhookProcessor`).

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** `EscalationNotifier` should only send the push notification and update `notified_at`. `EscalationTimeoutJob` scheduling should move to `WebhookProcessor`, alongside escalation creation.

**Current behavior:**
`EscalationNotifier#notify` has an `ensure` block that calls `@escalation.update!(notified_at: Time.current)` and `EscalationTimeoutJob.set(wait: ...).perform_later(@escalation.id)`. The timeout job is always scheduled, even if the push notification raises an error.

**Desired behavior:**
`EscalationNotifier#notify` sends the push notification and updates `notified_at`. It does not schedule any jobs. `WebhookProcessor#handle_escalation_triggered` (which creates the escalation and calls `EscalationNotifier`) is responsible for enqueuing `EscalationTimeoutJob` after the escalation is created and the notification is sent.

**Key interfaces:**
- `EscalationNotifier#notify` — remove the `ensure` block's `EscalationTimeoutJob` call; keep `notified_at` update; still rescue and log notification failures
- `WebhookProcessor#handle_escalation_triggered` — after `EscalationNotifier.notify`, enqueue `EscalationTimeoutJob.set(wait: TIMEOUT_SECONDS.seconds).perform_later(escalation.id)`
- The `TIMEOUT_SECONDS` constant — currently defined in both `EscalationNotifier` and `EscalationsController`; should live in one place (e.g. `EscalationTimeoutJob` or a config constant)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `EscalationNotifier` contains no reference to `EscalationTimeoutJob`
- [ ] `EscalationNotifier` specs do not need to assert job enqueue
- [ ] `WebhookProcessor#handle_escalation_triggered` enqueues `EscalationTimeoutJob` after notifying
- [ ] `EscalationTimeoutJob` is still enqueued even when the push notification fails (behavior preserved)
- [ ] `TIMEOUT_SECONDS` is defined in exactly one place
- [ ] Existing escalation flow behavior is unchanged end-to-end

**Out of scope:**
- Changing `EscalationTimeoutJob` implementation
- Changing the Pushover notification payload
- Changing `EscalationsController#reply` behavior

title: Extract GoalSummarizer from VapiAdapter: adapter should not call OpenAI
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

`VapiAdapter` states in its own comment: "Contains no business logic — only translation and HTTP." Yet it contains a `summarize_goal` method that makes an OpenAI API call to generate a short disclosure phrase. The adapter has two external dependencies (Vapi and OpenAI), can fail for OpenAI reasons, and violates its own stated contract.

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Extract `summarize_goal` out of `VapiAdapter` into a dedicated `GoalSummarizer` module. `VapiAdapter` should receive a pre-built disclosure phrase and never touch OpenAI.

**Current behavior:**
`VapiAdapter#first_message` calls `summarize_goal`, which instantiates an `OpenAI::Client`, makes a chat completion request, and falls back to a string truncation if OpenAI is unavailable. The adapter has two external HTTP dependencies.

**Desired behavior:**
A `GoalSummarizer` module (or class) exposes `.call(goal:) → String`, handling both the LLM path and the fallback. `VapiAdapter` accepts the summarized phrase as a parameter or receives a pre-built disclosure string. `VapiAdapter` makes no OpenAI calls.

**Key interfaces:**
- `GoalSummarizer.call(goal:) → String` — encapsulates LLM summarization and the truncation fallback; raises no external errors to callers (returns fallback on LLM failure)
- `VapiAdapter` — the `build_call_payload` / `first_message` methods should accept a pre-summarized goal phrase, or `GoalSummarizer` should be called upstream (in `PlaceCallJob`) before `VapiAdapter.call`
- `VapiAdapter.call` interface — may need a `goal_summary:` keyword argument, or the summarization happens before the adapter is invoked; either is acceptable
- `VapiAdapter` specs — should not require OpenAI stubs

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `VapiAdapter` contains no `OpenAI::Client` instantiation or chat completion calls
- [ ] `GoalSummarizer.call(goal:)` returns a short phrase suitable for spoken disclosure
- [ ] `GoalSummarizer` handles OpenAI unavailability gracefully (returns a fallback, does not raise)
- [ ] `GoalSummarizer` is independently testable without Vapi
- [ ] Disclosure behavior is unchanged end-to-end
- [ ] `VapiAdapter` specs do not stub OpenAI

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the Disclosure template wording
- Changing the OpenAI model used for summarization
- Extracting any other LLM calls from the system

title: Move PlaceCallJob scheduling out of CallPlan#approve! into the orchestration layer
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

`CallPlan#approve!` transitions the plan's state and then calls `PlaceCallJob.perform_later(id)`. This means a pure domain state transition directly orchestrates background job infrastructure. The policy "what happens when a CallPlan is approved" lives inside the model rather than at the point where approval is triggered.

This makes `CallPlan` harder to test in isolation (requires job infrastructure), and makes the side effect invisible at the call site.

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** `CallPlan#approve!` should own only the state transition; `PlaceCallJob` scheduling should move to the controller (or a thin orchestration layer) that calls `approve!`.

**Current behavior:**
`CallPlan#approve!` calls `update!(status: "approved", approved_at: Time.current)` and then `PlaceCallJob.perform_later(id)`. Every caller of `approve!` gets the job enqueue as a hidden side effect.

**Desired behavior:**
`CallPlan#approve!` only transitions state: `update!(status: "approved", approved_at: Time.current)`. The caller (e.g. `CallPlansController`) is responsible for enqueuing `PlaceCallJob` after `approve!` succeeds. This makes the orchestration explicit and visible at the call site.

**Key interfaces:**
- `CallPlan#approve!` — remove the `PlaceCallJob.perform_later` call; keep the guard against double-approval and the state transition
- `CallPlansController` (or wherever `approve!` is called) — add `PlaceCallJob.perform_later(call_plan.id)` after a successful `approve!`
- Any other callers of `approve!` — audit and update each one

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `CallPlan#approve!` contains no reference to `PlaceCallJob`
- [ ] `CallPlan` specs do not require `have_enqueued_job` assertions — state transition is testable in isolation
- [ ] All callers of `approve!` explicitly enqueue `PlaceCallJob` after the call
- [ ] Existing integration behavior is unchanged: approving a CallPlan still results in a call being placed
- [ ] Specs cover the controller-level enqueue

**Out of scope:**
- Introducing a service object or command pattern
- Changing `PlaceCallJob` implementation
- Changing the approval state machine or `AlreadyApprovedError`

title: Complete VoiceAgentProvider seam: add send_message to the interface contract
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

`VoiceAgentProvider` is the seam between the app and any voice provider adapter. Its contract currently covers only `.call(call_plan:)`. Mid-call message injection (`send_message`) is called directly via `VapiAdapter.send_message` from two places — `EscalationsController` and `EscalationTimeoutJob` — bypassing the abstraction entirely.

This means the seam is incomplete: a second voice provider adapter would need to know to implement `send_message` by reading callers, not the interface.

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Add `send_message(vapi_call_id:, message:)` to the `VoiceAgentProvider` module contract so all voice provider communication goes through a single seam.

**Current behavior:**
`VoiceAgentProvider` documents only `.call(call_plan:) → { call_id: String }`. `EscalationsController#reply` and `EscalationTimeoutJob#perform` call `VapiAdapter.send_message(...)` directly.

**Desired behavior:**
`VoiceAgentProvider` documents both `.call` and `.send_message`. All callers reference `VapiAdapter` only at the place where the adapter is chosen — not for specific methods. Callers that currently reference `VapiAdapter.send_message` directly are updated to go through the interface contract.

**Key interfaces:**
- `VoiceAgentProvider` module — add `send_message(vapi_call_id:, message:)` to the documented interface contract (the comment block defining what adapters must implement)
- `VapiAdapter.send_message` — implementation already exists; only the contract documentation and call sites change
- All callers of `VapiAdapter.send_message` — should be updated to use the adapter through whatever indirection the project already uses for `.call`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `VoiceAgentProvider` module comment documents `send_message(vapi_call_id:, message:)` as part of the interface contract
- [ ] No caller references `VapiAdapter` directly for `send_message` outside of adapter-selection logic
- [ ] `VoiceAgentProvider::ApiError` and `VoiceAgentProvider::PermanentError` are raised on failure (not adapter-specific errors)
- [ ] Existing specs pass; specs for `send_message` error paths are present

**Out of scope:**
- Introducing a runtime adapter registry or dependency injection container
- Changing the `VapiAdapter` implementation of `send_message`
- Adding any new voice provider adapters

title: Consolidate outcome extraction: ExtractOutcomeJob should delegate to OutcomeExtractor
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

There are two parallel LLM-based outcome extraction implementations:

- `OutcomeExtractor` — a service with a 6-field schema, structured system prompt, and JSON mode
- `ExtractOutcomeJob` — a job with its own 2-field schema, separate prompts, and its own OpenAI client setup

Neither uses the other. They diverge in schema, prompt design, and error handling. The answer to "how are Outcomes extracted?" has no single home.

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** `ExtractOutcomeJob` should delegate all LLM work to `OutcomeExtractor`, eliminating the duplicate implementation.

**Current behavior:**
`ExtractOutcomeJob` contains inline `VOICEMAIL_PROMPT` and `COMPLETED_PROMPT` constants, its own `ask_openai` method, and its own OpenAI client initialization. It produces a 2-field outcome hash `{ status, summary }`. `OutcomeExtractor` exists independently with a richer 6-field schema and is never called by the job.

**Desired behavior:**
`ExtractOutcomeJob` delegates to `OutcomeExtractor.call(transcript:, call_plan:)`. `OutcomeExtractor` handles both voicemail and completed sessions, returning a consistent Outcome hash. The job's only responsibility is finding the session, calling the extractor, and persisting the result.

**Key interfaces:**
- `OutcomeExtractor.call(transcript:, call_plan:)` — should handle both `voicemail` and `completed` session types, returning a hash with at minimum `status` and `summary` keys (and ideally the full 6-field schema already defined)
- `ExtractOutcomeJob#perform` — should be reduced to: load session, guard if outcome present, call `OutcomeExtractor`, persist result
- The Outcome hash schema should be defined once, in `OutcomeExtractor`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] `ExtractOutcomeJob` contains no LLM logic, no prompts, no OpenAI client initialization
- [ ] `ExtractOutcomeJob` delegates to `OutcomeExtractor.call(transcript:, call_plan:)` for both voicemail and completed sessions
- [ ] `OutcomeExtractor` handles the voicemail case (short summary) and completed case (full structured extraction)
- [ ] The outcome schema is consistent — same fields regardless of session type
- [ ] Existing specs for `OutcomeExtractor` and `ExtractOutcomeJob` pass; new specs cover the voicemail path in `OutcomeExtractor`

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the outcome schema stored on `CallSession#outcome`
- Changing retry/discard behavior on `ExtractOutcomeJob`
- Modifying how `ExtractOutcomeJob` is enqueued

title: Clarify form inputs
description:
Our form inputs for a new call need some clarity. 

We should add a contact name to the business section. We should try to confirm we have reached the correct business and person before proceeding. 

We should make it clear that the name is the name of the user, not the AI assistant.

title: Readme
description: Take a comprehensive look at this repo and make a great readme

title: Reuse a CallPlan: re-run an existing plan without re-entering details
description:
## What to build

Users want to place the same call again (e.g. follow up tomorrow) without recreating the entire CallPlan. A 'Run again' action should create a new CallSession from an existing approved CallPlan.

## Behavior

- Add 'Run again' button on the CallPlan show/detail view
- Creates a new CallSession and enqueues PlaceCallJob for the existing plan
- Does NOT require re-approval — plan is already approved
- Button hidden if a session is already in-progress (dialing/connected)
- New CallSession appears in the delegation history

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] 'Run again' button visible on an approved CallPlan with a completed/failed/voicemail session
- [ ] Creates new CallSession + enqueues PlaceCallJob
- [ ] Button hidden when a session is already active
- [ ] Specs cover controller action and job enqueueing

title: Voicemail-only numbers: skip live call and go straight to voicemail flow
description:
## What to build

Some phone numbers are known to always go to voicemail. Users should be able to mark a CallPlan target as voicemail-only, bypassing the consent opener.

## Behavior

- Add `voicemail_only` boolean to `CallPlan` (default: false)
- VapiAdapter uses a voicemail-optimized script when true: states identity, goal, and callback request without waiting for a response
- CallSession transitions directly to `voicemail` on `call.ended`
- UI: checkbox on CallPlan form labeled 'Number goes straight to voicemail'

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `voicemail_only` field on CallPlan model + migration
- [ ] VapiAdapter uses different firstMessage/system prompt when `voicemail_only` is true
- [ ] UI checkbox on call plan form
- [ ] Specs cover both paths

title: Retry a CallSession from existing CallPlan
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Allow a user to retry a call from an existing approved `CallPlan` — creating a new `CallSession` without rebuilding the CallPlan from scratch. Useful after voicemail, a failed call, or a declined call.

End-to-end: user views a completed/voicemail/failed CallSession → clicks "Retry call" → a new CallSession is created against the same CallPlan → `PlaceCallJob` is enqueued → call proceeds normally. The original CallSession is preserved with its Outcome.

- "Retry call" button visible on CallSession page for terminal states: `voicemail`, `failed`, `completed` (with `declined` outcome)
- Creates a new CallSession against the same CallPlan (no new approval needed — CallPlan is already approved)
- Enqueues `PlaceCallJob` for the new CallSession
- Original CallSession and Outcome are untouched
- Delegation history shows all CallSessions per Delegation (not just the latest)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] "Retry call" button appears on `voicemail`, `failed`, and `declined` CallSession pages
- [ ] Clicking retry creates a new CallSession referencing the same CallPlan
- [ ] New CallSession goes through the normal `queued → dialing → …` flow
- [ ] Original CallSession is not modified
- [ ] Delegation history shows multiple CallSessions per Delegation when retries exist
- [ ] Cannot retry a CallSession that is currently `in_progress` or `needs_user`
- [ ] Test covers: retry from voicemail, retry from failed, blocked retry on in-progress

## Blocked by

- #12 — Outcome display UI + Delegation history

title: Voicemail detection + voicemail outcome
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Handle the voicemail path: Vapi detects the call went to voicemail → agent leaves a message based on the CallPlan goal → CallSession transitions to `voicemail` → OutcomeExtractor runs → user sees a "Left voicemail" Outcome.

End-to-end: user's call goes to voicemail → agent leaves a short, purposeful message (not a sales pitch) → CallSession reaches `voicemail` state → Outcome shows "Left voicemail" with the message content → user can choose to retry.

- `voicemail` webhook event from Vapi triggers `voicemail` state transition
- VapiAdapter config includes a voicemail instruction: leave a brief message stating purpose and callback number (user's phone from CallPlan `allowed_to_share`)
- OutcomeExtractor handles `voicemail` state: `outcome.status = "voicemail"`, `outcome.summary` = what was left
- UI: CallSession page shows "Left voicemail" state with summary and a "Retry call" button
- Voicemail message is logged in transcript

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `voicemail` Vapi event transitions CallSession to `voicemail` state
- [ ] VapiAdapter config includes voicemail message instructions
- [ ] `ExtractOutcomeJob` is enqueued on `voicemail` state (same as `completed`)
- [ ] Outcome for voicemail has `status: "voicemail"` and a summary of what was left
- [ ] UI shows "Left voicemail" with summary and "Retry call" affordance
- [ ] Voicemail message content appears in transcript
- [ ] Test covers `voicemail` webhook → state transition → outcome shape

## Blocked by

- #8 — WebhookProcessor

title: Escalation reply handler: resume or fallback on timeout
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Handle the two paths after an Escalation is created: (1) user replies in time → agent resumes the call with the answer, (2) no reply within timeout → agent falls back to the `CallPlan#fallback` instruction.

End-to-end: user taps the reply URL from Pushover/SMS → submits yes/no or freeform reply → `Escalation#user_reply` saved → Vapi is instructed to resume with the answer. If `EscalationTimeoutJob` fires first → `Escalation#timed_out = true` → Vapi is instructed to execute the fallback.

- Reply endpoint (POST `/escalations/:id/reply`) — authenticated, token-protected link
- Stores `user_reply` and `resolved_at` on the Escalation
- Calls VapiAdapter to resume the call with the user's answer
- `EscalationTimeoutJob` (GoodJob) enqueued at `notified_at + timeout` (configurable, default 45s)
- On timeout: sets `timed_out = true`, calls VapiAdapter to execute `call_plan.fallback`
- If user replies after timeout has already fired: reply is saved but no further Vapi call is made
- CallSession transitions from `needs_user → in_conversation` on resume, or to `completed` if fallback ends the call

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Reply URL is unique per Escalation and requires authentication or a signed token
- [ ] Submitting a reply before timeout stores `user_reply`, sets `resolved_at`, and resumes the Vapi call
- [ ] `EscalationTimeoutJob` fires at the configured timeout
- [ ] On timeout: `timed_out = true`, Vapi executes `call_plan.fallback`
- [ ] Late reply (after timeout) is stored but does not trigger a second Vapi resume
- [ ] CallSession transitions correctly on resume (`needs_user → in_conversation`)
- [ ] Timeout duration is configurable via env/config (not hardcoded)
- [ ] Tests cover: reply before timeout, timeout with no reply, late reply after timeout

## Blocked by

- #13 — EscalationNotifier

title: EscalationNotifier: Pushover + SMS on needs_user
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

`EscalationNotifier` fires when a `CallSession` enters `needs_user`. It simultaneously sends a Pushover notification and a Twilio SMS to the user with the blocking question and a reply URL. The Escalation record is created to track the reply window.

End-to-end: Vapi sends `escalation.triggered` webhook with a blocking question → WebhookProcessor creates an `Escalation` record and calls `EscalationNotifier` → user receives both a Pushover push and an SMS within seconds → both messages contain the question and a link/reply path.

Schema (already defined in PRD):
```
escalations: id, call_session_id, question, notified_at,
             user_reply, resolved_at, timed_out (bool)
```

- `EscalationNotifier.notify(escalation:, user:)` sends both channels simultaneously (not sequentially)
- Pushover: title = "Call on hold", message = question + reply URL, high priority
- SMS (Twilio): "[Caller on hold] {question} — Reply here: {url}"
- Both fire even if one fails (rescue each independently, log errors)
- `Escalation#notified_at` set after both are dispatched
- Pushover API key and Twilio credentials from Rails credentials / env
- Both services stubbed in tests

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `EscalationNotifier` sends Pushover notification on escalation
- [ ] `EscalationNotifier` sends Twilio SMS on escalation
- [ ] Both are sent simultaneously, not sequentially
- [ ] If one channel fails, the other still fires
- [ ] `Escalation#notified_at` is set after dispatch
- [ ] Pushover message includes the blocking question and reply URL
- [ ] SMS includes the blocking question and reply URL
- [ ] Tests stub both Pushover and Twilio — no real API calls

## Blocked by

- #8 — WebhookProcessor

title: Outcome display UI + Delegation history
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Show users the structured Outcome after a call completes, and a full Delegation history page showing all past calls and their results.

End-to-end: user lands on a completed CallSession page and sees a clear summary, important details, follow-up flag, and full transcript. They can also visit a Delegation history page listing all past Delegations with outcomes at a glance.

- CallSession show page: outcome summary card, important_details list, follow_up_needed banner (if true), confidence indicator, full transcript (collapsible)
- Delegation index page: list of all user Delegations, each showing target name, date, outcome status, and one-line summary
- Turbo Stream updates Outcome section when `ExtractOutcomeJob` completes (replaces "Analysing call…" placeholder)
- `follow_up_needed: true` renders a prominent call-to-action ("You may need to follow up")

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Completed CallSession page shows summary, important_details, follow_up_needed, confidence, and transcript
- [ ] While Outcome is being extracted, page shows "Analysing call…" placeholder
- [ ] Turbo Stream replaces placeholder with Outcome when extraction completes
- [ ] Delegation history page lists all Delegations for the current user, newest first
- [ ] Each row shows: target name, date, outcome status badge, one-line summary
- [ ] `follow_up_needed: true` renders a distinct banner on the CallSession page
- [ ] `declined` outcome renders "Business declined AI call" (not "completed")

## Blocked by

- #11 — OutcomeExtractor

title: OutcomeExtractor: transcript → structured Outcome
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

`OutcomeExtractor` takes a completed transcript and the originating `CallPlan` and produces a structured `Outcome` that is persisted to `CallSession#outcome`. It runs as a background job after a CallSession reaches `completed` or `voicemail`.

End-to-end: Vapi sends `call.ended` → WebhookProcessor transitions to `completed` → `ExtractOutcomeJob` enqueued → `OutcomeExtractor` calls an LLM with the transcript + CallPlan goal/questions → structured Outcome written to the CallSession → UI updates.

Outcome shape:
```json
{
  "status": "completed",
  "outcome": "Car is ready for pickup.",
  "follow_up_needed": false,
  "summary": "Bob's Auto said the Honda Odyssey is ready. Total is $284.17. Pickup before 5:30 PM.",
  "important_details": ["Total: $284.17", "Pickup before 5:30 PM", "Bring card used for deposit"],
  "confidence": "high"
}
```

- `OutcomeExtractor` service: takes `transcript` (string) + `call_plan` → returns Outcome hash
- LLM prompt uses CallPlan `goal` and `questions_to_ask` as extraction targets
- `confidence` is `high | medium | low` based on how clearly the transcript answers the goal
- `ExtractOutcomeJob` (GoodJob) enqueued by WebhookProcessor on `completed` or `voicemail`
- LLM provider configurable via env (OpenAI by default)
- Tested with fixture transcripts — no real LLM calls in tests

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `OutcomeExtractor` returns a valid Outcome hash for a well-formed transcript
- [ ] `questions_to_ask` answers are surfaced in `important_details`
- [ ] `confidence` is set to `low` when the transcript does not clearly answer the goal
- [ ] `follow_up_needed` is `true` when the Outcome is ambiguous or incomplete
- [ ] `ExtractOutcomeJob` is enqueued by WebhookProcessor on `completed` and `voicemail`
- [ ] Outcome is persisted to `CallSession#outcome` after extraction
- [ ] Tests use fixture transcripts — no real LLM calls

## Blocked by

- #8 — WebhookProcessor

title: Disclosure enforcement + declined outcome handling
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Enforce that every CallSession opens with the mandatory Disclosure statement, and handle the case where the business declines to continue after hearing it (ADR-0003).

End-to-end: the VapiAdapter always injects the Disclosure instruction into every assistant config — there is no code path that omits it. When Vapi signals a `declined` event (business said no after disclosure), `WebhookProcessor` transitions the CallSession to `completed` and writes an Outcome with `status: "declined"`. The UI shows a clear "Business declined AI call" message.

- Disclosure instruction is a constant injected by `VapiAdapter` — not user-configurable
- `WebhookProcessor` handles a `call.declined` (or equivalent) Vapi event
- On declined: CallSession → `completed`, `outcome.status = "declined"`, `outcome.summary` = "The business declined to continue after the AI disclosure."
- `declined` is never retried with different framing (ADR-0003)
- UI: CallSession page shows a distinct "Declined" state with explanation and a suggestion to call manually

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every VapiAdapter-generated assistant config contains the Disclosure instruction verbatim
- [ ] No code path in VapiAdapter can produce a config without Disclosure
- [ ] `call.declined` webhook event transitions CallSession to `completed` with `outcome.status = "declined"`
- [ ] Declined CallSession is not re-enqueued or retried automatically
- [ ] UI renders a "Business declined AI call" state distinctly from `failed`
- [ ] Test asserts Disclosure string is present in every VapiAdapter config fixture

## Blocked by

- #8 — WebhookProcessor

title: Real-time CallSession status UI via Turbo Streams
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

A live CallSession status page that updates in real time as the call progresses — no polling, no page reloads. State changes from `WebhookProcessor` broadcast via Turbo Streams and appear in the browser instantly.

End-to-end: user approves a CallPlan and lands on the CallSession page. As Vapi webhooks arrive, the status badge updates (`Queued → Dialing → Connected → Completed`), and transcript chunks stream in live.

- CallSession show page with status badge, timeline, and transcript area
- Turbo Stream broadcasts triggered on every CallSession state transition
- Transcript area appends new chunks as `transcript.chunk` events arrive
- Status badge updates colour/label per state (e.g. green = completed, red = failed, amber = needs_user)
- Page is useful without JavaScript too (shows last-known state on load)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Navigating to a CallSession page shows current status without a page reload
- [ ] Status badge updates in the browser within 1s of a state transition
- [ ] Transcript chunks appear live as they are appended
- [ ] `needs_user` state shows an escalation banner prompting the user to check notifications
- [ ] `failed` state shows the error reason if available
- [ ] Page renders correctly with JS disabled (static state on load)

## Blocked by

- #8 — WebhookProcessor

title: WebhookProcessor: Vapi events → CallSession state transitions
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

A `WebhookProcessor` that receives incoming Vapi webhook events and drives `CallSession` state transitions. This is the bridge between the voice layer and the Rails control plane.

End-to-end: Vapi sends a `call.started` webhook → CallSession transitions to `connected`. Vapi sends `call.ended` → CallSession transitions to `completed` or `voicemail`. Transcript chunks are appended to `CallSession#transcript`. Escalation trigger → session enters `needs_user`.

- Rails webhook endpoint (POST `/webhooks/vapi`) with Vapi signature verification
- `WebhookProcessor` service object handles event routing
- Handled events: `call.started`, `call.connected`, `call.ended`, `transcript.chunk`, `escalation.triggered`
- Idempotent: delivering the same event twice produces the same state (no double-transition)
- Unknown events are logged and ignored (not 500)
- Webhook endpoint returns 200 quickly; heavy processing (OutcomeExtractor) is enqueued, not inline

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `call.started` → CallSession transitions to `connected`
- [ ] `call.ended` with answered → `completed`; with voicemail → `voicemail`
- [ ] `transcript.chunk` appends to `CallSession#transcript`
- [ ] `escalation.triggered` → `needs_user`
- [ ] Duplicate event delivery does not double-transition or duplicate transcript
- [ ] Invalid Vapi signature returns 401 and does not process the event
- [ ] Unknown event types return 200 and are logged
- [ ] Webhook controller tests use fixture payloads for each event type

## Blocked by

- #7 — PlaceCallJob

title: PlaceCallJob: approve CallPlan → enqueue → dial
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Wire the approval action to the background job queue. When a user approves a CallPlan, `PlaceCallJob` is enqueued. The job creates a `CallSession`, calls `VapiAdapter`, stores the returned `vapi_call_id`, and transitions the session to `dialing`.

End-to-end: user approves a CallPlan in the UI → CallSession appears in `queued` → transitions to `dialing` within seconds → `vapi_call_id` is stored on the CallSession.

- `PlaceCallJob` (GoodJob) created on CallPlan approval
- Job creates a CallSession (`queued`), calls VapiAdapter, stores `vapi_call_id`, transitions to `dialing`
- On adapter error: session transitions to `failed`, error message stored
- Retry with exponential backoff on transient errors (network, Vapi 5xx)
- No retry on permanent errors (invalid phone number, Vapi 4xx)
- `scheduled_at` support: if set on CallPlan, job is deferred until that time

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Approving a CallPlan enqueues `PlaceCallJob`
- [ ] Job creates a CallSession and transitions it `queued → dialing` on success
- [ ] `vapi_call_id` is persisted on the CallSession after successful dial
- [ ] On VapiAdapter error, CallSession transitions to `failed`
- [ ] Transient errors retry with backoff; permanent errors do not retry
- [ ] Scheduled calls are deferred until `scheduled_at`
- [ ] Job is idempotent — running it twice for the same CallPlan does not create two CallSessions

## Blocked by

- #6 — VoiceAgentProvider interface + VapiAdapter

title: VoiceAgentProvider interface + VapiAdapter (place the call)
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Define the `VoiceAgentProvider` interface and implement `VapiAdapter` as the Phase 1 concrete provider. The adapter translates a `CallPlan` into a Vapi assistant configuration and initiates an outbound call via the Vapi API.

End-to-end: given an approved CallPlan, `VapiAdapter.call(call_plan:)` creates a Vapi assistant with the correct goal, instructions, disclosure opening, forbidden actions, and fallback — then dials the target number. Returns a `vapi_call_id`.

The interface is the seam that lets Rails own conversation state in Phase 3 (ADR-0001). `VapiAdapter` contains no business logic — only translation and API calls.

Interface contract:
```ruby
# VoiceAgentProvider interface (duck-typed or via abstract class)
# .call(call_plan:) → { call_id: String }
# Any adapter must satisfy a shared contract test
```

Disclosure instruction injected into every adapter (ADR-0003):
> "Hi, I'm an AI assistant calling on behalf of [caller_name]. I'm calling to [goal]. Is it okay if I continue?"

If business says no → adapter must surface a `declined` signal to WebhookProcessor.

- `VoiceAgentProvider` base class or module with interface contract
- `VapiAdapter` implements it — maps CallPlan fields to Vapi assistant + call config
- Vapi API key stored in Rails credentials / env
- Shared contract test that any future adapter (PipecatAdapter, etc.) must pass
- `VapiAdapter` stubbed in test env — no real HTTP calls in tests

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `VapiAdapter.call(call_plan:)` returns a `vapi_call_id` string on success
- [ ] Vapi assistant config includes goal, questions, forbidden actions, fallback, and disclosure opening
- [ ] `disclose_ai` is always true — no code path omits the disclosure instruction
- [ ] Contract test exists; VapiAdapter passes it
- [ ] Vapi API errors are raised as a typed error class (not raw HTTP exceptions)
- [ ] No real Vapi API calls in the test suite (stubbed/mocked)

## Blocked by

- #5 — CallSession model + state machine

title: CallSession model + state machine
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Introduce the `CallSession` model with its full lifecycle state machine. A CallSession is the execution record for a single call attempt against an approved CallPlan.

End-to-end: a CallSession can be created for an approved CallPlan, transition through its states, and reach a terminal state. Invalid transitions are rejected. State changes broadcast via Turbo Streams.

Schema:
```
call_sessions: id, call_plan_id, status, vapi_call_id (nullable),
               transcript (text, nullable), outcome (jsonb, nullable),
               scheduled_at (nullable), started_at (nullable),
               ended_at (nullable), created_at
```

State machine:
```
drafted → queued → dialing → connected → in_conversation → completed
                                       → voicemail
                            → in_conversation → needs_user → in_conversation
                                                           → completed
                                                           → failed
drafted → failed  (on job error before dial)
```

- A CallSession can only be created from an `approved` CallPlan
- `vapi_call_id` set when Vapi confirms the call was initiated
- `outcome` jsonb: `{ status, outcome, summary, follow_up_needed, important_details, confidence }`
- `declined` is a valid `status` value inside `outcome.status` — CallSession itself reaches `completed`
- State changes trigger Turbo Stream broadcasts on the CallSession record

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All states and valid transitions are implemented and tested
- [ ] Invalid transitions raise a descriptive error
- [ ] A CallSession cannot be created against a `drafted` CallPlan
- [ ] Turbo Stream broadcast fires on every state transition
- [ ] `outcome` jsonb serializes and deserializes correctly including nested arrays
- [ ] `started_at` and `ended_at` are set automatically on the appropriate transitions
- [ ] State machine tests cover every valid path and at least three invalid transition attempts

## Blocked by

- #3 — Delegation + CallPlan models

title: Auto repair CallTemplate + CallPlan creation UI
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Ship the first product-owned `CallTemplate` — "Auto Repair Status Check" — and the UI that lets a user fill in its variables to produce a `CallPlan`, review it, and approve it.

End-to-end: user visits the dashboard, selects the auto repair template, fills in shop name, phone, their name, vehicle make/model, and any custom questions. They see a rendered CallPlan preview showing exactly what the agent will say and what it is forbidden to do. They approve it. The CallPlan is now in `approved` status.

- `CallTemplate` model (or seeded data) with: name, description, default goal, suggested questions, default forbidden actions, default fallback, variable schema
- Auto repair template seeded: goal = "Ask whether the vehicle is ready for pickup", forbidden = ["Approve new repairs", "Provide payment information"]
- CallPlan creation form driven by the template — variables rendered as labelled inputs
- CallPlan preview page: renders goal, allowed_to_share, questions, forbidden_actions, fallback in human-readable form
- Approve button triggers `drafted → approved` transition
- Hotwire-friendly — no full page reloads needed

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Auto repair CallTemplate exists (seeded or hardcoded)
- [ ] User can select the template and fill in variables to create a CallPlan
- [ ] CallPlan preview renders all fields clearly before approval
- [ ] Approve button transitions status to `approved`; UI reflects new state
- [ ] Required fields (shop name, phone, caller first name, vehicle) are validated before submission
- [ ] User can edit a drafted CallPlan before approving
- [ ] Approved CallPlan shows read-only view — no edit affordance

## Blocked by

- #3 — Delegation + CallPlan models

title: Delegation + CallPlan models, schema, and drafted→approved state
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Introduce the two core domain models — `Delegation` and `CallPlan` — with their schema, validations, and the `drafted → approved` state transition. A Delegation is the user-facing container; a CallPlan is the approved specification for what the agent will say and do.

End-to-end: a user can create a Delegation with a CallPlan (via Rails console or a minimal form), inspect all fields, and approve it. An approved CallPlan cannot be re-drafted. An unapproved CallPlan cannot proceed to a CallSession (enforced at the model layer).

Schema:
```
delegations:  id, user_id, call_template_id (nullable), created_at
call_plans:   id, delegation_id, target_name, target_phone, caller_name,
              goal, allowed_to_share (jsonb), questions_to_ask (jsonb),
              allowed_decisions (jsonb), forbidden_actions (jsonb),
              fallback, status (drafted|approved), approved_at, created_at
```

- `caller_name` always required (user's first name minimum)
- `disclose_ai` is always true — not stored as a column, enforced as a constant
- `status` transitions: `drafted → approved` only; no reversal
- `approved_at` set on transition

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `Delegation` and `CallPlan` models exist with correct schema and associations
- [ ] A CallPlan in `drafted` status can be approved; `approved_at` is set
- [ ] An already-approved CallPlan cannot be approved again (raises or returns error)
- [ ] `target_phone`, `caller_name`, and `goal` are validated as present
- [ ] `allowed_to_share`, `questions_to_ask`, `allowed_decisions`, `forbidden_actions` serialize/deserialize as arrays via jsonb
- [ ] Model-layer tests cover valid transitions, invalid transitions, and validations

## Blocked by

- #2 — Rails app skeleton

title: Rails app skeleton: auth, Postgres, GoodJob, Hotwire
description:
## Parent

#1 — PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant

## What to build

Bootstrap the Rails monolith that will serve as the control plane for all Delegations and CallSessions. This slice establishes the full working skeleton every subsequent slice builds on: authentication, database, background jobs, and real-time UI infrastructure.

End-to-end: a logged-in user can reach a dashboard page that loads, shows a placeholder, and has a working Turbo Stream connection. A GoodJob worker processes a smoke-test job. Nothing breaks on deploy.

- Rails 8 app with Postgres
- Devise (or equivalent) for user authentication — login, logout, protected routes
- GoodJob configured and running as the background job queue

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately

title: Encrypt notification_channels credentials at rest
description:
notification_channels table stores pushover_user_key and telegram_chat_id as plain strings. Add AR Encryption (encrypts :column) to both fields once encryption keys are configured in credentials.

## Acceptance
- Both columns use Rails encrypts
- Test env has encryption keys configured
- Existing rows migrated

title: Render template variable_schema as guided form fields on the call plan creation screen
description:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Background

Each `CallTemplate` has a `variable_schema` — an ordered list of labelled, typed inputs that define exactly what information the agent needs to execute that template (e.g. shop name, vehicle, patient name). Today those fields are **ignored** during call plan creation. The user faces a blank \"Goal\" textarea and has to figure out on their own what to fill in, with no template-specific guidance.

## Problem

The call plan form (`app/views/call_plans/_form_fields.html.erb`) shows the same generic fields regardless of template:
- \"Who are we calling?\" (target_name, target_phone, contact name)  
- \"Caller identity\" (caller_name)  
- \"Instructions for the AI\" (freetext goal + fallback)

None of this is wired to the template's `variable_schema`. The four seeded templates each define 4-6 required fields that carry specific labels and context (e.g. *\"Vehicle (year, make, model)\"* for Auto Repair, *\"Patient's full name\"* for Pharmacy). Without surfacing those, users must already know what to type.

## Desired Behaviour

When a delegation has an associated `CallTemplate`, the new call plan form should:

1. **Render one labelled input per `variable_schema` entry**, in schema order, instead of (or alongside) the generic fields.  
   - Use the entry's `"label"` as the field label.  
   - Mark fields with `"required": true` as required.  
   - For well-known keys (`target_phone`) use the appropriate input type (`telephone_field`); everything else defaults to `text_field`.

2. **Show a short contextual hint beneath each field** so the user knows why it's needed and what format is expected. Examples (from the existing seeds):
   | Template | Key | Suggested hint |
   |---|---|---|
   | Auto Repair | `vehicle` | *e.g. "2019 Honda Civic" — the agent will use this to identify your car* |
   | Pharmacy | `patient_name` | *Full name as it appears on the prescription* |
   | Restaurant | `preferred_time` | *e.g. "7:00 PM" — the agent will try this time and offer to accept ±1 hour* |
   | Home Service | `service_type` | *e.g. "HVAC tune-up" or "leaking kitchen faucet"* |

3. **Populate the call plan's `goal` field automatically** by interpolating the collected variable values into the template's `goal_template` string (or display the pre-filled goal as read-only if no interpolation markers are present yet).

4. **Keep the generic form for delegations with no template** (custom calls still need the freetext goal).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Selecting any of the 4 seeded templates and clicking \"Use this template\" leads to a form that shows only/primarily the schema-driven fields, not a blank goal textarea.
- [ ] Each field carries its schema label and a helpful hint.
- [ ] Required fields are visually marked and fail Rails/HTML5 validation if blank.
- [ ] Submitting the form creates a valid `CallPlan` with a meaningful goal string derived from the template + entered values.
- [ ] Delegations with no template still render the existing generic form unchanged.
- [ ] No regressions: existing call plan edit flow continues to work.

## Files Likely Involved

- `app/views/call_plans/_form_fields.html.erb` — main form partial
- `app/views/call_plans/new.html.erb` — already passes `@template` to the view
- `app/models/call_template.rb` — `variable_schema` attribute lives here
- `app/controllers/call_plans_controller.rb` — may need to expose schema to form
- `db/seeds.rb` — reference for all four schemas and goal_template strings

## Out of Scope (for this issue)

- Editing `variable_schema` via the UI (admin-only concern)
- Goal interpolation with `{{mustache}}` syntax — acceptable to pre-fill the full goal string verbatim if no markers exist yet
- Adding new templates

title: PRD: AI-Powered Outbound Personal Calling Assistant
description:
## Problem Statement

People have a growing backlog of life-admin calls they keep putting off — checking if the car is ready, leaving a prescription refill message, asking a contractor about availability. These calls are short, repetitive, and stressful to make. The friction isn't the call itself; it's the interruption, the hold time, and the cognitive load of being present for something that doesn't require a human.

## Solution

An AI-powered outbound calling assistant that lets users delegate these calls. The user describes the call, approves a structured CallPlan, and the AI agent places the call, completes the task within explicit boundaries, and delivers a structured Outcome. The user never picks up the phone.

The product is transparent (always discloses AI), constrained (never acts outside the approved CallPlan), and async (the user gets a summary after, not a live experience).

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want to select a CallTemplate (e.g. "check repair status") so that I don't have to write a CallPlan from scratch.
2. As a user, I want to fill in the blanks on a CallTemplate (business name, phone, my name, vehicle details) so that the CallPlan is personalised to my situation.
3. As a user, I want to review and approve a CallPlan before any call is placed so that I remain in control of what the agent says and does.
4. As a user, I want to see exactly what facts the agent is allowed to share so that I trust it won't overshare my personal information.
5. As a user, I want to define forbidden actions (e.g. "do not approve new repairs") so that the agent cannot take actions I haven't authorised.
6. As a user, I want to define a fallback instruction (e.g. "ask them to call me directly") so that the agent handles dead ends gracefully.
7. As a user, I want the agent to always identify itself as AI calling on my behalf so that I'm never impersonating myself or deceiving the business.
8. As a user, I want to receive a push notification and SMS if the call is placed on hold for an escalation so that I can respond within the hold window.
9. As a user, I want to reply to an escalation notification with a yes/no decision so that the agent can resume without me joining the call.
10. As a user, I want to receive a structured Outcome after every call — status, summary, important details, follow-up needed — so that I know exactly what happened.
11. As a user, I want to see the full call transcript so that I can verify what the agent said.
12. As a user, I want to know if the business declined the AI call so that I can decide whether to call myself.
13. As a user, I want to retry a CallSession from the same CallPlan if the first attempt went to voicemail so that I don't have to rebuild the call from scratch.
14. As a user, I want to see all my past Delegations and their Outcomes so that I have an audit trail of calls made on my behalf.
15. As a user, I want the agent to leave a voicemail if no one answers so that the call isn't wasted.
16. As a user, I want CallSession status updates (dialing, connected, completed) reflected in real time in my browser so that I know the call is progressing.
17. As a user, I want to customise a CallTemplate's defaults (add questions, restrict allowed facts) so that I can tailor it to my preferences.
18. As a user, I want to schedule a call for a specific time (e.g. "call after 9am") so that I don't have to be available to trigger it.
19. As a user, I want to be notified immediately if a CallSession fails so that I can investigate or retry.
20. As a user, I want the agent to never provide payment information, even if asked, so that I'm protected from social engineering.

## Implementation Decisions

### Modules

**CallTemplate** — Product-owned blueprint per vertical (auto repair, prescription, home service). Defines default goal, suggested questions, and safe boundaries. Users fill in variables; power users can override defaults. Templates are the primary onramp — custom CallPlans are the escape hatch.

**CallPlan** — Rails model. Stores: target (name, phone), identity (caller name, disclose_ai always true), goal, allowed_to_share, questions_to_ask, allowed_decisions, forbidden_actions, fallback. Status: `drafted` → `approved`. Must be approved before a CallSession can be created.

**CallSession** — Rails model with state machine. States: `drafted → approved → queued → dialing → connected → voicemail | in_conversation → needs_user | completed | failed`. Owns transcript and Outcome. Multiple CallSessions can reference one CallPlan (retries). Turbo Streams broadcast state changes for real-time UI.

**Outcome** — Value object / JSON column on CallSession. Fields: `status`, `outcome`, `summary`, `follow_up_needed`, `important_details[]`, `confidence`. `declined` is a valid status (business refused AI call) and is `completed`, not `failed`.

**Disclosure** — Required behaviour enforced by VoiceAgentProvider interface. Every CallSession opening must include: AI identification, user's first name, call purpose, soft consent ask. If business declines, agent records `declined` immediately without negotiation.

**VoiceAgentProvider** — Abstract interface: `call(call_plan:, audio_stream:, tools:, safety_rules:)`. Phase 1–2: VapiAdapter. Phase 3: PipecatAdapter or LiveKitAdapter. Designed so Rails can own conversation state in Phase 3 without restructuring the call lifecycle.

**VapiAdapter** — Concrete Phase 1–2 implementation. Translates CallPlan into Vapi assistant config, initiates outbound call. No business logic here.

**WebhookProcessor** — Receives Vapi webhook events, drives CallSession state transitions. Idempotent. Validates webhook signatures.

**OutcomeExtractor** — Takes completed transcript + CallPlan, produces structured Outcome via LLM. Runs after `completed` or `voicemail`. Assigns confidence level.

**EscalationNotifier** — Fires when CallSession enters `needs_user`. Sends Pushover + Twilio SMS simultaneously. Includes blocking question and reply URL. Falls back to CallPlan `fallback` on timeout.

**PlaceCallJob** — GoodJob job. Enqueued on CallPlan approval. Calls VapiAdapter. Handles retries with backoff. Supports scheduled/deferred execution.

### Architectural decisions

- Rails monolith + Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus). Real-time CallSession status via Turbo Streams over Action Cable.
- GoodJob (Postgres-backed) for background jobs. No Redis.
- Vapi as Phase 1 voice provider; replaced in Phase 3 via VoiceAgentProvider abstraction seam.
- Conversation state owned by Vapi in Phase 1–2; Rails takes over in Phase 3.
- Escalation is async: hold → notify (Pushover + SMS) → wait → resume or fallback.
- No voice cloning. No live call transfer for MVP.

### Schema sketch

```
delegations:     id, user_id, call_template_id, created_at
call_plans:      id, delegation_id, target_name, target_phone, caller_name, goal,
                 allowed_to_share (jsonb), questions_to_ask (jsonb),
                 allowed_decisions (jsonb), forbidden_actions (jsonb),
                 fallback, status, approved_at, created_at
call_sessions:   id, call_plan_id, status, vapi_call_id, transcript (text),
                 outcome (jsonb), scheduled_at, started_at, ended_at, created_at
escalations:     id, call_session_id, question, notified_at,
                 user_reply, resolved_at, timed_out (bool)
```

## Testing Decisions

Good tests verify external behaviour, not implementation details.

- **CallSession state machine** — every valid and invalid transition. Do not test internal Vapi calls.
- **OutcomeExtractor** — fixture transcripts → known expected Outcomes. Assert on structured output fields.
- **EscalationNotifier** — both Pushover and SMS sent on trigger. Timeout fallback behaviour. Mock external services.
- **VoiceAgentProvider interface contract** — VapiAdapter satisfies the interface; any future adapter must pass the same contract tests.
- **WebhookProcessor** — idempotency (same event twice → same state). Signature validation rejects tampered payloads.

## Out of Scope

- Voice cloning or the agent sounding like the user
- Live call transfer / bridging the user into the active call
- Native mobile app (web-first; mobile push via Pushover app)
- Medical refill calls for MVP (compliance risk — later vertical)
- Inbound call handling
- Multi-user / team accounts
- Campaign or bulk outbound calling
- Phase 3 voice runtime (Pipecat / LiveKit Agents)

## Further Notes

**The moat is the delegation framework, not the voice model.** CallPlans, CallTemplates, escalation, Outcomes, audit trail, and trust are the differentiating layer. The voice provider is a swappable commodity.

**First CallTemplate to ship:** auto repair / service status check. Low compliance risk, high frequency, clear success condition.

**Disclosure is non-negotiable.** Every CallSession must open with AI identification + user's first name + purpose. A declined call is `completed` / `declined` — never retried with different framing.

**Phase roadmap:**
- Phase 1: Rails + Vapi + Twilio + single CallTemplate (auto repair) + Pushover/SMS escalation
- Phase 2: More CallTemplates, scheduled calls, retry flows, Outcome polish
- Phase 3: Replace Vapi with Pipecat/LiveKit, Rails owns conversation state

See `CONTEXT.md` for canonical terminology and `docs/adr/` for all architectural decisions.
